why-most-components-are-too-flexible.title: Why Most Components Are Too Flexible?
why-most-components-are-too-flexible.excerpt: Not every form of flexibility leads to reuse. Sometimes trying to support every possible scenario makes components harder to understand, maintain, and evolve.

angular-21-small-changes-that-matter.title: Angular 21: Small Changes That Matter
angular-21-small-changes-that-matter.excerpt: Why Angular's latest release feels less like a revolution and more like another step toward simpler, more predictable frontend development.

frontend-is-mostly-about-data.title: Frontend Is Mostly About Data, Not Interfaces
frontend-is-mostly-about-data.excerpt: After a few years building real-world applications, you realize most frontend complexity doesn't come from components or CSS. It comes from data.

frontend-war.title: React vs Angular: The Wrong Frontend War
frontend-war.excerpt: After working with both frameworks professionally, I've come to believe the real winner isn't React or Angular. It's TypeScript.

frontend-arch.title: Frontend Architecture: Complexity Is a Debt, Not a Feature
frontend-arch.excerpt: Most frontend architecture problems don't come from a lack of structure. They come from adding structure before reality demands it.

localhost-cors-explained.title: If Everything Is Running on Localhost, Why Do We Still Get CORS Errors?
localhost-cors-explained.excerpt: A practical explanation of origins, browser security, and why localhost applications can still trigger CORS errors.

the-most-expensive-frontend-problem-is-c.title: The Most Expensive Frontend Problem Is Confusion
the-most-expensive-frontend-problem-is-c.excerpt: Bugs can be fixed. Confusion can survive for years. And in many cases, it costs teams far more than any individual technical problem.

every-frontend-team-eventually-builds-a-.title: Every Frontend Team Eventually Builds a Design System
every-frontend-team-eventually-builds-a-.excerpt: You may not call it a design system, but the moment components, patterns, and decisions start being shared, one is already taking shape.
